audit_checks.prd_trace: Refunds under 100 USD can be processed immediately.
audit_checks.setup: Support agent submits refund below 100 USD.

cleanup_requirements: remove created refund or use isolated test DB

audit_checks.steps_or_method: Create refund and verify immediate processing path.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: Refund enters existing processing path without manager approval.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Low-value refund is blocked by approval or fails processor handoff.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Create refund threshold path and processor handoff.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Refunds at or above 100 USD require manager approval; high-value requests cannot bypass approval.
audit_checks.steps_or_method: Create high-value refund and inspect status plus processor calls.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: Refund is pending_approval and processor is not called before approval.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: High-value refund reaches processing/completed or processor before manager approval.
required_evidence: processor mock showing zero pre-approval calls
audit_checks.retest_scope: Threshold logic, API authorization, processor gate.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Managers can approve with comments; approved requests trigger existing refund processor.
audit_checks.setup: Support manager approves pending refund with comment.

cleanup_requirements: reset processor mock and refund records

audit_checks.steps_or_method: Approve refund; inspect state, audit event, and processor call count.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: Approval persists comment/actor, audit event exists, processor triggers exactly once after durable approval.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Missing audit/comment, wrong-state approval succeeds, processor triggers early or more than once.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Approval endpoint/action, audit write, processor integration.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Managers can reject with comments; rejected requests notify support agent.
audit_checks.setup: Support manager rejects pending refund with comment.

negative_cases: missing required comment if implementation requires it

audit_checks.steps_or_method: Reject refund; inspect state, audit event, processor non-call, notification event.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: Refund is rejected, audit exists, processor is not called, support-agent notification is emitted.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Notification missing/wrong recipient, audit missing, processor called, or wrong-state rejection succeeds.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Rejection endpoint/action, audit write, notification path.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Status lifecycle: draft, pending_approval, approved, rejected, processing, completed, failed.
audit_checks.setup: Exercise valid and invalid refund state transitions.
audit_checks.steps_or_method: Run transition tests and inspect status rendering.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: All PRD states are supported distinctly and invalid transitions fail safely.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Missing/collapsed state or invalid mutation succeeds.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Status model, transition handlers, UI status rendering.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Derived quality standard from high-risk refund processor trigger and manager approval actions.
audit_checks.setup: Retry duplicate approval/rejection/submission and simulate concurrent manager actions.

cleanup_requirements: reset processor mock and audit store

audit_checks.steps_or_method: Issue duplicate/concurrent requests and count final states, audit events, and processor calls.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: One durable outcome and no duplicate refund processing.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Duplicate processor calls, multiple terminal decisions, or inconsistent final state.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Approval/rejection handlers, concurrency controls, processor enqueue logic.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Status lifecycle includes failed; approved requests trigger existing refund processor.
audit_checks.automation_level: Required when processor test double is available
audit_checks.setup: Approve high-value refund and force processor failure/timeout.

positive_cases: approved refund with mocked processor failure

audit_checks.steps_or_method: Simulate processor failure and inspect state, audit history, logs, and duplicate prevention.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: Failed state is visible, audit remains intact, and no duplicate refund is issued.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Hidden stuck state, lost audit, duplicate refund, or missing diagnostic evidence.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Processor integration, failure handling, history/status display, retry/idempotency.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Support manager approves/rejects high-value refunds; role definitions.
audit_checks.automation_level: Required negative tests plus code review
audit_checks.steps_or_method: Review route/action guards and auth tests.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: API/server enforces role permissions independent of UI.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Approval mutation can be performed by unauthorized role or only hidden in UI.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Approval and rejection actions are audited.
audit_checks.setup: Inspect status update and audit write implementation.
audit_checks.steps_or_method: Review transaction boundaries and audit records.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: No terminal approval/rejection state exists without matching audit event.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Audit write is best-effort without recovery or lacks actor/comment/timestamp.

audit_checks.prd_trace: Approved requests trigger existing refund processor; high-value cannot bypass approval.
audit_checks.setup: Inspect all processor entry points and queue producers/consumers.
audit_checks.steps_or_method: Trace processor callers and verify approval gate.
audit_checks.pass_criteria: High-value refunds are processor-eligible only after approval.
audit_checks.fail_criteria: Any path processes high-value refund without durable approval.
audit_checks.retest_scope: Create path, approve path, processor workers.

blocked_questions.question: What channel, content, SLA, and retry policy are required for rejection notifications?
blocked_questions.impact: Exact delivery/content tests cannot be unconditional.

blocked_questions.question: What idempotency key or concurrency contract should create/approve/reject expose?
blocked_questions.impact: Audit can verify no duplicates, but exact API contract is undefined.

blocked_questions.question: What retry/reconciliation behavior is required after third-party failure?
blocked_questions.impact: Failure visibility can be tested; retry policy remains conditional.
